content:
The Problem

I wanted to learn cybersecurity seriously.

So I did what everyone does — opened

Google, YouTube, Reddit. Within an hour

I had 30+ tabs, three contradicting

roadmaps, and more confusion than

when I started.

I wasn't learning cybersecurity.

I was drowning in resources about

learning cybersecurity.

Closed everything. Sat in silence.

Thought — there has to be a better way.

The Solution

I built CyberPath Academy.

A free, structured platform that answers

one question clearly:

"Where do I start?"

https://cyberpathacademy.org

What's Inside

• 12-month SOC Analyst roadmap (complete)

• Digital Forensics track (8 months)

• OSINT 30-day mini course

• AI study mentor (Claude API)

• Daily quizzes with weak-spot tracking

• XP system, streaks, heatmap calendar

• CV builder, CTF tracker, interview prep

• Command library, skill radar

• Zero signup. Zero paywall. Forever free.

The Build Stats

• Built solo in 16 days

• Pure HTML, CSS, JavaScript only

• Zero frameworks

• Zero budget

• 1-2 hours per day after office

• Built from Kathmandu, Nepal 🇳🇵

The Stack

Language → HTML, CSS, JavaScript

Framework → None (zero)

Hosting → Vercel (free)

Domain → cyberpathacademy.org

Cost → $0 to build

Database → None (localStorage only)

The Story

The hardest part wasn't the code.

It was content. Writing 12 months of

structured SOC curriculum while:

• Working full time at office

• Studying CCNA simultaneously

• Attending 3rd semester university

Some days I had 30 minutes.

Some days 3 hours.

Some days I watched Messi score

a hat-trick instead. No regrets.

One day I went to Mustang, Nepal

with friends and closed the laptop

completely for 5 days.

Came back and wrote 3 months of

content in 24 hours.

The Current Stats (3 days after launch)

• 85+ users

• 7 countries (US, Nepal, Poland,
France, Netherlands, Germany, Malaysia)

• #1 on Google for "CyberPath Academy"

• Zero paid marketing

What I Learned

• Pure JS is powerful enough for
complex apps — don't always need React

• Content is 10x harder than code

• SEO matters from day 1

• Ship first, perfect later

• Consistency beats talent

Why Free Forever

Because the confusion I felt on Day 1

doesn't care about your budget.

Students in Nepal, India, Bangladesh,

Nigeria, Brazil — everywhere — face

the same overwhelming internet and

the same question:

"Where do I start?"

CyberPath exists to answer that.

Clearly. Completely. Freely. For everyone.

Try It

https://cyberpathacademy.org

Would love honest feedback from

the DEV community:

• What's missing?

• What would you add?

• What could be better?

Built with purpose. Free for everyone
